old_claim.note: Ancestor Worship is contextual infrastructure or shared knowledge, not a strict hard prerequisite.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains from ritual_burial_practices to ancestor_worship; the Qafzeh evidence supports intentional mortuary treatment with ocher but does not establish belief in influential dead relatives.

source_shape.source_locator: Intentional burial section: describes Qafzeh human remains, red ocher, ocher-stained stone tools, ritual inference, and age; it does not claim ancestor worship.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports intentional burial and ritual pigment association without identifying ancestor veneration as the mechanism.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Ancestor worship is a narrower religious-social claim than intentional burial, so retaining it would launder an unsupported belief system into the Qafzeh mortuary evidence.

ontology_before: The burial node treated ancestor worship as a direct common dependency for ritual interment.
ontology_after: The burial node preserves mortuary treatment evidence without requiring ancestor-veneration belief systems.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: ancestor_worship no longer points directly into ritual_burial_practices.
would_reject_if: A source directly connected the earliest scoped Qafzeh burial evidence to ancestor veneration.
The node were narrowed to later ancestor-cult funerary rites rather than early intentional burial.
The ancestor_worship node were itself resourced to the same mortuary evidence and renamed to avoid overclaiming.
short_reason: Intentional burial evidence does not justify a direct ancestor-worship prerequisite.
